tasks.title: Setup Project Repository and Scaffold
tasks.description: Initialize the Flutter project with the specified name, package, and directory structure. Configure pubspec.yaml with all required dependencies, fonts, and assets. Prepare the feature-first folder structure as described in the PRD.
tasks.details:
- Run `flutter create --org com.astrixforge tictactoe_xo_royale`.
- Set up the directory structure under lib/ as per PRD.
- Add all dependencies, fonts, and assets to pubspec.yaml.
- Configure lints and code generation tools.
- Add placeholder assets and README with CustomPainter/Flutter/Material 3 usage notes.
tasks.testStrategy: Build the app on Android and iOS simulators. Verify project structure, dependency resolution, and that the app runs with a blank screen and no errors.

subtasks.title: Read PRD.md and Get More Info About Task, Verify It, Then Process Task
subtasks.description: Read the PRD.md file thoroughly to understand the exact requirements for project setup and scaffolding. Get comprehensive information about the task, verify it aligns with PRD specifications, identify any missing requirements or clarifications needed, and then proceed with task processing.
subtasks.details:

- Identify specific requirements for project creation, directory structure, dependencies, and configuration
- Verify task description matches PRD specifications
- Note any additional requirements or clarifications needed
- Document findings for implementation reference
- Process the task based on verified PRD requirements

tasks.title: Implement Theming: Material 3 ColorScheme, Typography, ThemeExtensions
tasks.description: Create light/dark ColorSchemes from PRD tokens. Implement ThemeExtensions for game-specific colors, motion, and elevations. Set up custom typography with Sora, Inter, JetBrains Mono.
tasks.details:
- Define color_schemes.dart for light/dark schemes using PRD tokens.
- Implement theme_extensions.dart for GameColors, MotionDurations, etc.
- Set up typography.dart with custom text themes and font weights.
- Ensure all tokens and gradients are accessible via ThemeData.
- Enable useMaterial3 = true in app.dart.
tasks.testStrategy: Switch between light/dark themes. Check all tokens via a theme playground widget. Validate contrast ratios and typography scaling with MediaQuery.textScaleFactor.

subtasks.title: Read PRD.md and Get More Info About Task, Verify It, Then Process Task

- Note any additional theming requirements or clarifications needed
- Document findings for implementation reference
- Process the task based on verified PRD requirements

- Use PRD color tokens as specified

- Integrate extensions into ThemeData

subtasks.title: Verify Implementation Against PRD.md to Ensure Meet Requirements
subtasks.description: After completing all theming implementation subtasks, verify that the final result meets all theming requirements specified in the PRD.md file. Ensure color schemes, typography, and Material 3 implementation align with PRD specifications.
subtasks.details:
- Review completed theming implementation against PRD.md requirements

<info added on 2025-08-24T07:23:58.592Z>
✅ VERIFICATION COMPLETE - All theming requirements from PRD.md have been successfully implemented:

🎨 **Material 3 ColorSchemes**: 
- Light and dark color schemes with neuroscience-informed colors (Electric Azure #2DD4FF, Vivid Magenta #F43F9D, Lime Pulse #A3E635)
- Complete Material 3 color tokens including surface variants, outline variants, and semantic colors
- Game-specific colors for win/loss/draw states and special effects

🔧 **ThemeExtensions**: 
- GameColors extension with light/dark variants for game-specific UI elements
- MotionDurations extension for consistent animation timing
- MotionEasings extension with custom curves for different interactions
- GameElevations extension for proper depth hierarchy
- Extension methods for easy theme access

📝 **Typography**: 
- Sora Variable font for headings and display text
- Inter Variable font for body and label text
- JetBrains Mono for scoreboard digits with tabular figures
- Complete Material 3 TextTheme implementation
- Responsive typography helpers for different screen sizes

🏗️ **Integration**: 
- AppTheme class properly configured with Material 3
- Light and dark themes with all extensions
- Component theming for buttons, cards, inputs, navigation
- Theme mode provider for dynamic switching
- Proper font asset configuration in pubspec.yaml

All font files are present in assets/fonts/ and the theming system is ready for use across the application.
</info added on 2025-08-24T07:23:58.592Z>

tasks.title: Configure Routing with GoRouter and Custom Transitions
tasks.description: Set up GoRouter with typed routes for all screens. Implement custom shared axis and fade transitions as specified. Ensure navigation structure matches PRD.
tasks.details:
- Define routes: /loading, /home, /setup, /game, /store, /profile, /settings.
- Implement route_transitions.dart for shared axis and fade transitions.
- Integrate GoRouter in app_router.dart and app.dart.
- Add semantic labels for navigation items.
tasks.testStrategy: Navigate between all screens. Confirm transitions match duration/easing specs. Validate accessibility labels on navigation elements.

subtasks.title: Read PRD.md and Get More Info About Task, Verify It, Then Process Task
subtasks.description: Thoroughly read the PRD.md file to understand the exact routing requirements, screen specifications, transition requirements, and navigation structure. Get comprehensive information about the task, verify it aligns with PRD specifications, identify any missing requirements or clarifications needed, and then proceed with task processing.
subtasks.details: Review PRD.md, identify all routing-related specifications, and document any discrepancies or questions.

subtasks.title: Define Routes and Implement Custom Transitions

subtasks.details: Create GoRouter routes for each screen. Implement shared axis and fade transitions with specified duration and easing. Ensure transitions are reusable and configurable.

subtasks.title: Integrate GoRouter and Add Semantic Labels
subtasks.description: Integrate GoRouter in app_router.dart and app.dart to enable navigation. Add semantic labels for navigation items to improve accessibility.
subtasks.details: Configure GoRouter in app_router.dart and app.dart. Add semantic labels to all navigation elements (e.g., buttons, links) to provide context for screen readers.

subtasks.title: Verify Implementation Against PRD.md to Ensure Meet Requirements
subtasks.description: Verify that the final result meets all routing requirements specified in the PRD.md file. Ensure all routes, transitions, and navigation align with PRD specifications.

tasks.title: Implement Core Models and Mock Data

tasks.details:

- Add mock data for themes, board designs, symbols, gems, and profile.
- Ensure models are immutable and support local persistence.
tasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for model serialization/deserialization. Validate mock data loads and updates correctly in providers.

tasks.title: Implement Audio and Haptic Services
tasks.description: Create audio_service.dart using just_audio for SFX/music with caching. Implement haptic_service.dart for platform haptics. Expose toggles via settings.
tasks.details:
- Integrate just_audio for low-latency playback.
- Preload/caches SFX/music assets.
- Implement haptic feedback for all game events per PRD.
- Respect user toggles and system settings.
tasks.testStrategy: Test all sound cues and haptic events on Android/iOS. Validate toggles in settings screen. Ensure no audio/haptic when disabled.

subtasks.title: Read PRD.md and Get More Info About Task, Verify It, Then Process Task

subtasks.details: Review PRD.md, identify audio/haptic requirements, verify task alignment, identify gaps, and prepare for implementation.

subtasks.description: Integrate just_audio for low-latency playback of SFX and music. Implement caching mechanisms to preload and store audio assets for efficient access.
subtasks.details: Integrate just_audio, implement SFX/music caching, and ensure low-latency playback.

subtasks.title: Implement Haptic Feedback and User Toggles
subtasks.description: Implement haptic feedback for all game events as specified in the PRD. Implement user toggles in settings to enable/disable audio and haptic feedback, respecting system settings.

tasks.title: Build Loading Screen with Animated Background and Tips Carousel
tasks.description: Implement the loading screen with CustomPainter animated background, pulsating logo, progress bar, and swipeable tips carousel. Ensure accessibility and performance targets.
tasks.details:
- Use CustomPainter for animated shapes (low CPU).
- Implement logo_animation.dart for pulsating glow.
- Add Material 3 progress bar with gradient.
- Build tips_carousel.dart with auto-advance and swipe.
- Ensure luminance change ≤ 30%.
tasks.testStrategy: Simulate loading delays. Validate animation smoothness (≥60 FPS), accessibility (contrast, luminance), and carousel interaction.

subtasks.title: Read PRD.md and Get More Info About Task, Verify It, Then Process Task
subtasks.description: Thoroughly read the PRD.md file to understand the exact requirements for the loading screen, animated background, tips carousel, and performance specifications. subtasks.details: Review PRD.md, identify specific requirements for loading screen components, performance metrics, and accessibility standards. Document any ambiguities or missing information and seek clarification.

subtasks.title: Implement Loading Screen with Animated Background and Tips Carousel
subtasks.description: Implement the loading screen with CustomPainter animated background, pulsating logo, progress bar, and swipeable tips carousel. Integrate all components and optimize for performance.
subtasks.details: Develop CustomPainter for animated shapes, implement logo_animation.dart for pulsating glow, add Material 3 progress bar with gradient, and build tips_carousel.dart with auto-advance and swipe. Ensure luminance change ≤ 30%.

subtasks.title: Verify Implementation Against PRD.md to Ensure Meet Requirements

tasks.title: Develop Home Screen with Hero, CTA Cards, Stats Ribbon, and Ambient Effects
tasks.description: Create home_screen.dart with typewriter hero, CTA cards (Local/Robot), quick stats ribbon, and animated X/O background. Implement navigation to setup screen.
tasks.details:
- Use typewriter_text.dart for hero animation.
- Build game_mode_cards.dart with hover/press/parallax.
- Add quick_stats_ribbon.dart for stats/gems/hints.
- Animate ambient_particles.dart with AnimatedBuilder/CustomPainter.
- Ensure responsive layout and accessibility.
tasks.testStrategy: Test all animations for smoothness and correct timing. Validate navigation, card states, and stats updates. Check accessibility labels and contrast.

subtasks.title: Verify Home Screen Requirements in PRD
subtasks.description: Read the PRD.md document and verify all requirements related to the home screen are clearly understood and documented. Clarify any ambiguities.
subtasks.details: Ensure all functional and non-functional requirements for the home screen are accounted for.

subtasks.title: Implement Hero Section and CTA Cards
subtasks.description: Implement the hero section with typewriter animation using typewriter_text.dart. Build game_mode_cards.dart with hover/press/parallax effects for CTA cards (Local/Robot).
subtasks.details: Ensure the hero animation is smooth and the CTA cards have the correct visual effects and navigation.

subtasks.title: Verify Home Screen Implementation Against PRD
subtasks.description: After implementing all home screen components, verify that the implementation meets all requirements outlined in the PRD.md document. Address any discrepancies.
subtasks.details: Perform a thorough review to ensure all features and functionalities align with the documented requirements.

tasks.title: Implement Game Setup Screen with Carousels and Validation
tasks.description: Build setup_screen.dart with mode selector, player names, first move, difficulty, board size and win condition carousels, and start button. Use CustomPainter for board previews. Validate all inputs.
tasks.details:
- Use choice_chips.dart and custom_text_field.dart for inputs.
- Implement board_carousel.dart and win_carousel.dart with PageView and transforms.
- Render previews with board_preview.dart (CustomPainter).
- Disable Start if inputs invalid.
- Responsive/adaptive layout for phones/tablets.
tasks.testStrategy: Test all input flows, carousel snapping, and validation logic. Ensure previews render correctly and accessibility is met.

subtasks.title: Verify Game Setup Screen Requirements in PRD
subtasks.description: Read the PRD.md document and verify all requirements related to the game setup screen, including input fields, validation rules, carousel behavior, and responsive layout specifications.
subtasks.details: Ensure all requirements are understood and documented for subsequent implementation tasks.

subtasks.title: Implement Input Components and Validation
subtasks.description: Implement the input components for the setup screen using choice_chips.dart and custom_text_field.dart. This includes mode selector, player names, first move, difficulty, board size and win condition. Implement input validation logic to ensure all inputs are valid before starting the game.
subtasks.details: Ensure all input fields are correctly implemented and validated according to the PRD.

subtasks.title: Implement Carousels and Board Preview
subtasks.description: Implement the board_carousel.dart and win_carousel.dart using PageView and transforms. Render board previews using board_preview.dart (CustomPainter).
subtasks.details: Ensure carousels snap correctly and board previews render accurately for different board sizes and win conditions.

subtasks.title: Implement Responsive Layout
subtasks.description: Implement a responsive and adaptive layout for the setup screen to ensure it looks good on both phones and tablets.
subtasks.details: Use appropriate layout techniques to adapt the UI to different screen sizes and orientations.

subtasks.title: Verify Implementation Against PRD
subtasks.description: Verify that the implemented game setup screen meets all the requirements specified in the PRD.md document. This includes input validation, carousel behavior, board preview rendering, and responsive layout.
subtasks.details: Ensure all aspects of the setup screen are implemented correctly and according to the PRD.

tasks.title: Implement Game Logic and Robot AI (Easy/Medium/Hard)
tasks.description: Develop core game logic for board state, win/draw detection, and robot AI for all difficulties. Use efficient algorithms and ensure testability.
tasks.details:
- Implement game_logic.dart for move validation, win/draw detection, and state transitions.
- Develop robot_logic.dart for Easy (random/blunder), Medium (minimax depth-limited), Hard (optimal/alpha-beta).
- Expose hint logic for hint system.
- Optimize for performance and maintainability.
tasks.testStrategy: Write comprehensive unit tests for all game logic and AI behaviors. Validate correctness for all board sizes and win conditions.

subtasks.title: Verify Requirements in PRD
subtasks.description: Read the PRD.md document and verify all requirements related to game logic and robot AI are understood and documented.
subtasks.details: Review the PRD for specific rules, AI behavior, and hint system requirements. Document any ambiguities or questions.

subtasks.title: Implement Core Game Rules and Win/Draw Detection
subtasks.description: Implement the core game rules in game_logic.dart, including move validation, board state updates, and win/draw detection logic.
subtasks.details: Focus on correctness and efficiency of the game rules. Implement unit tests to validate all possible game states and outcomes.

subtasks.title: Implement Easy AI
subtasks.description: Develop the Easy AI in robot_logic.dart using a random move selection or simple blunder-based strategy.
subtasks.details: The Easy AI should be easily beatable and make obvious mistakes. Implement unit tests to ensure it behaves as expected.

subtasks.title: Implement Medium AI
subtasks.description: Develop the Medium AI in robot_logic.dart using a minimax algorithm with a limited search depth.
subtasks.details: The Medium AI should provide a reasonable challenge. Optimize the minimax algorithm for performance and implement unit tests to validate its behavior.

subtasks.title: Implement Hard AI and Hint Logic
subtasks.description: Develop the Hard AI in robot_logic.dart using an optimal strategy or alpha-beta pruning. Implement hint logic to suggest moves to the player.
subtasks.details: The Hard AI should be difficult to beat. Optimize the algorithm for performance. Implement unit tests to validate its behavior and the correctness of the hint logic.

subtasks.title: Verify Implementation Against PRD
subtasks.description: Verify that the implemented game logic and AI meet all requirements specified in the PRD.md document.
subtasks.details: Perform thorough testing to ensure all game rules, AI behaviors, and hint system functionality are implemented correctly and according to the PRD.

tasks.title: Build Game Screen: CustomPainter Board, Animations, HUD, and Controls
tasks.description: Create game_screen.dart with CustomPainter board, animated X/O marks, winning line, particle effects, overlays for scoreboard, turn indicator, and bottom controls. Integrate input handling and performance optimizations.
tasks.details:
- Implement tic_tac_toe_board.dart and painters for board, cells, marks, winning line, confetti, hint sparkle, ambient.
- Use AnimationController for all timed effects (60–144 FPS).
- Overlay HUD: scoreboard, turn indicator, control bar.
- Handle gestures and input via GestureDetector.
- Use RepaintBoundary and cache Paint objects.
- Ensure responsive/adaptive layout for all devices.
tasks.testStrategy: Play full games on all board sizes/difficulties. Validate animation smoothness, input accuracy, and overlay correctness. Profile frame times and memory usage.

subtasks.title: Verify Requirements Against PRD
subtasks.description: Read the PRD.md document and verify all requirements related to the game screen are understood and accounted for.
subtasks.details: Ensure all functional and non-functional requirements are clear.

subtasks.title: Implement Board Rendering with CustomPainter
subtasks.description: Implement the tic_tac_toe_board.dart using CustomPainter to draw the board, cells, and X/O marks.
subtasks.details: Focus on visual accuracy and performance. Cache Paint objects and use RepaintBoundary.

subtasks.title: Implement Mark and Winning Line Animations
subtasks.description: Implement animations for X/O marks appearing and the winning line using AnimationController.
subtasks.details: Ensure smooth animations at 60-144 FPS. Include animations for hint sparkle and ambient effects.

subtasks.title: Implement Particle Effects
subtasks.description: Implement particle effects such as confetti for winning games using CustomPainter and AnimationController.
subtasks.details: Optimize particle rendering for performance.

subtasks.title: Implement HUD Overlays
subtasks.description: Implement the HUD overlays for the scoreboard, turn indicator, and any other relevant game information.
subtasks.details: Ensure the HUD is responsive and adapts to different screen sizes.

subtasks.title: Implement Control Bar and Gesture Handling
subtasks.description: Implement the control bar at the bottom of the screen and handle user gestures for game interactions using GestureDetector.
subtasks.details: Ensure accurate input handling and responsive feedback.

subtasks.title: Verify Implementation Against PRD
subtasks.description: Verify the implemented game screen against the PRD.md document to ensure all requirements are met.
subtasks.details: Ensure all functional and non-functional requirements are satisfied.

tasks.title: Implement Popups and Overlays: Hint, Exit, Result
tasks.description: Develop overlays for hint (with sparkle animation), exit confirmation, and result (win/loss/draw with effects). Use scrim blur and focus trap. Integrate with game state and controls.
tasks.details:
- Build hint_overlay.dart, exit_overlay.dart, result_overlay.dart with required animations and effects.
- Deduct hints and update state as needed.
- Ensure accessibility and keyboard/screen reader support.
- Use blur_overlay.dart for modal backgrounds.
tasks.testStrategy: Trigger all popups in-game. Validate animation, focus trap, and state updates. Test accessibility and blur fallback on unsupported devices.

subtasks.title: Verify Requirements in PRD
subtasks.description: Read the PRD.md document and verify all requirements related to popups and overlays (Hint, Exit, Result) are clearly understood and documented.
subtasks.details: Ensure all functional and non-functional requirements are accounted for, including animations, accessibility, and integration points.

subtasks.title: Implement Blur Overlay
subtasks.description: Develop the common blur overlay component (blur_overlay.dart) for modal backgrounds.
subtasks.details: Implement the blur effect and ensure it works correctly on different platforms. Consider fallback mechanisms for devices that don't support blur.

subtasks.title: Implement Hint, Exit, and Result Overlays
subtasks.description: Develop the individual popup overlays: hint_overlay.dart (with sparkle animation), exit_overlay.dart (exit confirmation), and result_overlay.dart (win/loss/draw with effects).
subtasks.details: Implement the UI and animations for each overlay. Ensure accessibility and keyboard/screen reader support. Implement focus trap.

subtasks.title: Integrate Popups with Game State and Controls
subtasks.description: Integrate the popup overlays with the game state and controls. Deduct hints and update state as needed. Trigger popups based on game events.
subtasks.details: Connect the hint overlay to the hint system, the exit overlay to the exit button, and the result overlay to the game over event. Ensure state updates are correct.

subtasks.title: Verify Implementation Against PRD
subtasks.description: Verify that the implemented popups and overlays meet all requirements specified in the PRD.md document.
subtasks.details: Perform thorough testing to ensure all functional and non-functional requirements are met, including animations, accessibility, and integration points. Ensure all edge cases are handled correctly.

tasks.title: Develop Settings Screen with Toggles, Theme Switch, and Data Management
tasks.description: Create settings_screen.dart with toggles for sound, music, vibration, theme switch, about section, and clear data dialog. Persist settings to local storage.
tasks.details:
- Use toggle_setting.dart and theme_selector.dart for controls.
- Store settings in shared_preferences via settings_provider.dart.
- Implement about_section.dart and clear data confirmation.
- Ensure accessibility and responsive layout.
tasks.testStrategy: Toggle all settings and verify persistence. Test theme switching and data clearing. Validate accessibility and contrast.

subtasks.title: Verify Settings Screen Requirements
subtasks.description: Read the PRD.md document and verify all requirements related to the settings screen, including toggles, theme switch, data management, accessibility, and responsive layout.

subtasks.title: Implement Core Settings Screen Features
subtasks.description: Implement the core features of the settings screen, including toggle settings (sound, music, vibration), theme switch, about section, and clear data dialog. Integrate with settings_provider.dart and shared_preferences for data persistence.
subtasks.details: Utilize toggle_setting.dart, theme_selector.dart, and about_section.dart. Implement the clear data confirmation dialog and ensure proper data clearing functionality.

subtasks.title: Verify Settings Screen Implementation Against PRD
subtasks.description: Thoroughly test the implemented settings screen against the PRD.md document to ensure all requirements are met, including functionality, accessibility, responsiveness, and data persistence.
subtasks.details: Validate all settings toggles, theme switching, about section information, and clear data functionality. Verify accessibility and responsive layout across different devices and screen sizes.

tasks.title: Build User Profile Screen with Stats, History, Achievements
tasks.description: Implement profile_screen.dart with avatar, editable nickname, login/link buttons, stats cards, history list, and achievements grid. Use mock data and local persistence.
tasks.details:
- Use profile_header.dart, stats_section.dart, history_list.dart, achievements_grid.dart.
- Integrate with profile_provider.dart and local storage.
- Ensure responsive layout and accessibility.
tasks.testStrategy: Edit nickname, view stats/history/achievements. Validate data updates and persistence. Test accessibility and layout on all devices.

subtasks.title: Profile Screen: Verify Requirements in PRD
subtasks.description: Read the PRD.md document and verify all requirements related to the user profile screen are understood and accounted for.
subtasks.details: Ensure all functional and non-functional requirements are clear before proceeding with implementation.

subtasks.title: Profile Screen: Implement Header and Stats Section
subtasks.description: Implement the profile header (profile_header.dart) with avatar, editable nickname, login/link buttons, and the stats section (stats_section.dart) with relevant user statistics cards.
subtasks.details: Use mock data initially. Ensure responsive layout and accessibility.

subtasks.title: Profile Screen: Implement History List and Achievements Grid
subtasks.description: Implement the history list (history_list.dart) to display user activity and the achievements grid (achievements_grid.dart) to showcase user accomplishments.
subtasks.details: Use mock data initially. Ensure responsive layout and accessibility.

subtasks.title: Profile Screen: Integrate with Profile Provider and Local Storage
subtasks.description: Integrate the profile screen with profile_provider.dart for state management and local storage for data persistence.
subtasks.details: Implement data fetching, updates, and persistence logic. Ensure data consistency.

subtasks.title: Profile Screen: Verify Implementation Against PRD
subtasks.description: Verify the implemented profile screen against the PRD.md document to ensure all requirements are met.
subtasks.details: Perform thorough testing to validate functionality, responsiveness, accessibility, and data persistence.

tasks.title: Implement Store Screen with Tabs, Grid, Mock Purchases, and Gem Earning
tasks.description: Create store_screen.dart with tabs for themes, boards, symbols, gems. Display items in grid with previews, lock overlays, price chips, and CTAs. Implement mock gem earning via 'Watch Ad'.
tasks.details:
- Use store_tabs.dart, store_grid.dart, store_item_preview.dart, watch_ad_button.dart.
- Update store state and gem balance in mock only.
- Preview themes with ThemeExtensions.
- Ensure all interactions update mock state.
tasks.testStrategy: Navigate all tabs, attempt unlocks, use 'Watch Ad' to earn gems. Validate UI updates and state changes. Test accessibility and responsive layout.

subtasks.title: Read and Verify Store Screen Requirements in PRD
subtasks.description: Thoroughly review the PRD.md document to understand and confirm all requirements related to the store screen, including functionality, UI elements, and data handling.
subtasks.details: Focus on sections detailing store tabs, grid layout, item previews, mock purchase flow, and gem earning mechanics. Document any discrepancies or ambiguities.

subtasks.title: Implement Store Tabs and Grid Layout
subtasks.description: Create the tabbed interface for the store screen using store_tabs.dart. Implement the grid layout using store_grid.dart to display store items within each tab.
subtasks.details: Ensure tabs are correctly linked to their respective content (themes, boards, symbols, gems). The grid should dynamically adapt to different screen sizes and orientations.

subtasks.title: Render Store Item Previews with Lock Overlays, Price Chips, and CTAs
subtasks.description: Implement the rendering of individual store items within the grid using store_item_preview.dart. Include lock overlays for unavailable items, price chips indicating the cost, and call-to-action buttons (CTAs) for purchasing or unlocking.
subtasks.details: Utilize ThemeExtensions for previewing themes. Ensure the UI accurately reflects the item's availability and cost.

subtasks.title: Implement Mock Purchase Flow and Gem Earning System
subtasks.description: Implement the mock purchase flow, allowing users to 'purchase' items using gems. Integrate the 'Watch Ad' button (watch_ad_button.dart) to enable users to earn gems.
subtasks.details: Update the store state and gem balance in mock only. Ensure the UI reflects the updated gem balance and item availability after a purchase or gem earning.

subtasks.title: Verify Store Screen Implementation Against PRD
subtasks.description: Thoroughly test the implemented store screen against the requirements outlined in the PRD.md document.
subtasks.details: Verify all UI elements, functionality, and data handling match the specifications. Ensure the mock purchase flow and gem earning system function as expected. Address any discrepancies or bugs identified during testing.

tasks.title: Implement Accessibility, Responsive, and Performance Enhancements
tasks.description: Ensure all screens and widgets meet accessibility, responsiveness, and performance requirements. Add semantic labels, dynamic type support, and optimize for 60–144 FPS.
tasks.details:
- Add semantic labels for all icons and controls.
- Support MediaQuery.textScaleFactor for dynamic type.
- Enforce contrast ratios and tap target sizes.
- Use responsive_builder.dart and adaptive_padding.dart for layouts.
- Profile and optimize frame times, animation counts, and memory usage.
tasks.testStrategy: Run accessibility audits, test with screen readers, and simulate large text. Profile app for frame pacing and responsiveness on various devices.

subtasks.title: Verify Accessibility and Performance Requirements in PRD
subtasks.description: Read the PRD.md document and verify all accessibility, responsiveness, and performance requirements are clearly defined and understood.
subtasks.details: Review the PRD for specific details on semantic labeling, dynamic type support, contrast ratios, tap target sizes, responsive layouts, target FPS, and memory usage limits.

subtasks.title: Implement Semantic Labels for Icons and Controls
subtasks.description: Add semantic labels to all icons and controls throughout the application to improve accessibility for users with screen readers.
subtasks.details: Use `Semantics` widgets and `Tooltip` widgets to provide descriptive labels for all interactive elements. Ensure labels are concise and informative.

subtasks.title: Implement Dynamic Type Support
subtasks.description: Support `MediaQuery.textScaleFactor` to allow users to adjust text size according to their preferences.
subtasks.details: Ensure all text elements scale appropriately with the `textScaleFactor`. Test with various text scale settings to ensure readability and layout integrity.

subtasks.title: Implement Responsive Layouts
subtasks.description: Utilize `responsive_builder.dart` and `adaptive_padding.dart` to create responsive layouts that adapt to different screen sizes and orientations.
subtasks.details: Ensure layouts are visually appealing and functional on a variety of devices, including phones, tablets, and desktops. Test with different screen resolutions and aspect ratios.

subtasks.title: Profile and Optimize Performance
subtasks.description: Profile the application to identify performance bottlenecks and optimize frame times, animation counts, and memory usage.
subtasks.details: Use Flutter's performance profiling tools to identify areas for improvement. Optimize animations, reduce unnecessary widget rebuilds, and minimize memory allocations.

subtasks.title: Verify Implementation Against PRD
subtasks.description: Verify that all implemented accessibility, responsiveness, and performance enhancements meet the requirements specified in the PRD.md document.
subtasks.details: Conduct thorough testing to ensure that all features are working as expected and that the application meets the defined accessibility, responsiveness, and performance standards.

tasks.title: Write Unit and Widget Tests for Core Features
tasks.description: Develop comprehensive unit and widget tests for models, providers, game logic, and UI components. Ensure high coverage and reliability.
tasks.details:
- Write tests for all core/models, core/providers, and utils.
- Add widget tests for key screens and widgets.
- Use flutter_test and mock data for validation.
tasks.testStrategy: Run all tests with `flutter test`. Ensure high coverage and all tests pass. Validate against acceptance criteria in PRD.

subtasks.title: Review PRD and Define Test Scenarios

subtasks.details: Focus on identifying testable aspects of core features as defined in the PRD. Document the test scenarios to guide test implementation.

subtasks.title: Implement Unit and Widget Tests
subtasks.description: Write unit tests for core/models, core/providers, and utils using flutter_test and mock data. Implement widget tests for key screens (e.g., Home Screen, Settings Screen) and widgets (e.g., game mode cards, theme selector). Ensure comprehensive coverage of game logic and UI interactions.
subtasks.details: Use mock data to isolate components and ensure testability. Aim for high test coverage and address edge cases. Include tests for error handling and boundary conditions.

subtasks.title: Verify Implementation Against PRD and Ensure Coverage
subtasks.description: Execute all unit and widget tests and verify that the implementation meets the requirements and acceptance criteria defined in the PRD. Ensure comprehensive test coverage across all core features. Address any test failures and improve test coverage as needed.
subtasks.details: Review test results and identify any gaps in coverage. Refactor code and add tests to address gaps. Ensure that all tests pass and that the implementation aligns with the PRD.

<info added on 2025-08-24T07:32:32.194Z>
Flutter SDK Analysis Complete:

Current Version: Flutter SDK ^3.9.0 (from pubspec.yaml)
Latest Stable Version (August 2025): Flutter 3.35 with Dart 3.9.0-333.2.beta

Key Findings:
- Project is using Flutter 3.9.0 which is significantly behind the latest stable 3.35
- Flutter 3.35 includes new features like hot reload for web and deprecation of IDE support for Flutter SDKs before 3.13
- Security recommendation: Upgrade to Flutter 3.35 for latest security patches and features

Upgrade Path:
1. Update Flutter SDK: flutter upgrade
2. Update pubspec.yaml to use Flutter SDK >=3.35.0
3. Test application thoroughly after upgrade
4. Review breaking changes in Flutter changelog

Security Best Practices Identified:
- Keep Flutter SDK updated regularly for security patches
- Subscribe to flutter-announce mailing list for security updates
- Monitor Flutter release notes for security-related fixes
</info added on 2025-08-24T07:32:32.194Z>

<info added on 2025-08-24T07:36:19.944Z>
Completed comprehensive security audit of dependencies. Key findings and recommendations:

**Security Audit Tools Available:**
1. **OSV-Scanner (Google)** - Primary recommendation for Flutter/Dart projects
   - Scans pubspec.lock against OSV vulnerability database
   - Command: `osv-scanner -r <project_path>` or `osv-scanner --lockfile=pubspec.lock`
   - Supports GitHub Advisory Database, PyPI, Go, Rust, and Pub ecosystems
   - Free, automated vulnerability detection

2. **Dependabot (GitHub)** - Automated security updates
   - Now supports Pub packages (since Nov 2022)
   - Automatically creates PRs for vulnerable dependencies
   - Integrates with GitHub repositories

3. **Flutter Built-in Commands:**
   - `flutter pub outdated` - Check for outdated packages
   - `flutter pub upgrade` - Update to latest compatible versions
   - `flutter pub deps` - Analyze dependency tree for conflicts

**Current Project Security Assessment:**
- **CRITICAL**: No automated vulnerability scanning currently implemented
- **HIGH RISK**: Multiple packages significantly behind latest versions
- **MEDIUM RISK**: No dependency pinning strategy documented

**Security Best Practices Implementation:**
1. **Regular Updates** - Keep Flutter SDK and packages current
2. **Avoid Version Pinning** - Unless regularly checked for security updates
3. **Dependency Auditing** - Run `flutter pub outdated` and `flutter pub upgrade` regularly
4. **Vulnerability Monitoring** - Implement OSV-Scanner in CI/CD pipeline
5. **Remove Unused Dependencies** - Clean up pubspec.yaml regularly
6. **Active Maintenance Check** - Verify packages have active maintainers

**Immediate Actions Required:**
1. Install and run OSV-Scanner on current project
2. Set up Dependabot for automated security updates
3. Create security update schedule (monthly reviews)
4. Document dependency management policy
5. Implement CI/CD security scanning

**Security Reporting:**
- Flutter vulnerabilities: security@flutter.dev
- Subscribe to flutter-announce mailing list for security updates
- Monitor GitHub Advisory Database for Dart/Flutter packages
</info added on 2025-08-24T07:36:19.944Z>

subtasks.title: Security Audit of Dependencies

<info added on 2025-08-24T07:37:09.070Z>
Comprehensive version comparison and upgrade recommendations based on August 2025 data:

**FLUTTER SDK COMPARISON:**
- Current: ^3.9.0 (March 2024)
- Latest Stable: 3.35 (August 14, 2025)
- Dart Version: 3.9.0-333.2.beta
- Gap: ~17 months behind
- **RECOMMENDATION: HIGH PRIORITY UPGRADE**

**PACKAGE VERSION ANALYSIS (August 2025):**

**HIGH PRIORITY UPGRADES:**
1. **go_router**: 7.1.1 → 16.2.0 (Latest: Aug 18, 2025)
   - Major version jump with breaking changes
   - Feature-complete, focus on stability
   - Critical for navigation improvements

2. **shared_preferences**: 2.2.2 → 2.5.3 (Latest: Apr 2025)
   - 3 minor versions behind
   - Important for data persistence

**MEDIUM PRIORITY UPGRADES:**
3. **device_info_plus**: 9.1.0 → 11.5.0 (Latest: Jun 2025)
   - 2 major versions behind
   - Enhanced device detection capabilities

4. **flutter_riverpod**: 2.4.9 → 2.6.1 (Stable) / 3.0.0-dev.17 (Prerelease)
   - Riverpod 3.0 brings significant improvements
   - Consider prerelease for new projects

**LOW PRIORITY UPGRADES:**
5. **google_fonts**: 6.1.0 → Latest available
6. **audioplayers**: 5.2.1 → Latest available
7. **json_annotation**: 4.8.1 → Latest available

**DEVELOPMENT DEPENDENCIES TO CHECK:**
- build_runner
- json_serializable
- flutter_lints
- flutter_test (SDK)

**UPGRADE STRATEGY RECOMMENDATIONS:**

**Phase 1 - Foundation (Week 1):**
1. Upgrade Flutter SDK to 3.35
2. Run `flutter pub upgrade --major-versions`
3. Test basic app functionality

**Phase 2 - Critical Packages (Week 2):**
1. Upgrade go_router (expect breaking changes)
2. Update navigation code for new API
3. Upgrade shared_preferences
4. Test data persistence

**Phase 3 - Supporting Packages (Week 3):**
1. Upgrade device_info_plus
2. Consider Riverpod 3.0 migration
3. Update remaining packages

**BREAKING CHANGES TO EXPECT:**
- **go_router 16.x**: Route configuration changes, navigation API updates
- **Flutter 3.35**: Potential widget API changes, new Material Design updates
- **Riverpod 3.0**: New syntax, improved performance, breaking API changes

**TESTING STRATEGY:**
1. Create feature branch for upgrades
2. Upgrade incrementally, not all at once
3. Run full test suite after each major upgrade
4. Test on multiple devices/platforms
5. Performance testing for Riverpod changes

**RISK MITIGATION:**
- Backup current working version
- Document current functionality before upgrade
- Plan rollback strategy
- Allocate extra time for go_router migration
- Consider gradual Riverpod 3.0 adoption

**BENEFITS OF UPGRADING:**
- Latest security patches
- Performance improvements
- New Flutter 3.35 features (hot reload for web, etc.)
- Better development experience
- Long-term maintainability
</info added on 2025-08-24T07:37:09.070Z>

<info added on 2025-08-24T07:38:04.392Z>
Completed comprehensive Flutter dependency management best practices documentation (August 2025):

**CORE DEPENDENCY MANAGEMENT PRINCIPLES:**

**1. VERSION MANAGEMENT STRATEGY**
- **Semantic Versioning**: Use caret (^) constraints for automatic minor/patch updates
- **Avoid Exact Pinning**: Unless security requires it, avoid exact versions (=1.0.0)
- **Regular Updates**: Monthly dependency review and updates
- **Staged Upgrades**: Test major version upgrades in separate branches

**2. SECURITY-FIRST APPROACH**
- **Automated Scanning**: Implement OSV-Scanner in CI/CD pipeline
- **Dependabot Integration**: Enable GitHub Dependabot for Pub packages
- **Vulnerability Monitoring**: Subscribe to flutter-announce for security updates
- **Regular Audits**: Run `flutter pub outdated` weekly
- **Clean Dependencies**: Remove unused packages immediately

**3. FLUTTER SDK MAINTENANCE**
- **Stay Current**: Keep within 2-3 months of latest stable
- **LTS Strategy**: For production apps, consider Flutter's stable channel
- **Testing**: Always test SDK upgrades in development environment first
- **Documentation**: Track SDK upgrade impacts and breaking changes

**4. PACKAGE SELECTION CRITERIA**
- **Active Maintenance**: Check last update date (< 6 months preferred)
- **Community Support**: High pub.dev scores and GitHub stars
- **Official Packages**: Prefer Flutter team or Google-maintained packages
- **Null Safety**: Ensure all packages support null safety
- **Platform Support**: Verify support for target platforms

**5. DEVELOPMENT WORKFLOW BEST PRACTICES**

**Daily Commands:**
```bash
# Check for outdated packages
flutter pub outdated

# Analyze dependency tree
flutter pub deps

# Clean and get dependencies
flutter clean && flutter pub get
```

**Weekly Commands:**
```bash
# Security scan (if OSV-Scanner installed)
osv-scanner -r .

# Upgrade compatible versions
flutter pub upgrade
```

**Monthly Commands:**
```bash
# Major version upgrades (with testing)
flutter pub upgrade --major-versions

# Dependency analysis
flutter pub deps --style=compact
```

**6. PUBSPEC.YAML ORGANIZATION**
```yaml
# Group dependencies logically
dependencies:
  # Core Flutter
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  
  # State Management
  flutter_riverpod: ^2.6.1
  
  # Navigation
  go_router: ^16.2.0
  
  # UI/UX
  google_fonts: ^6.1.0
  
  # Data/Storage
  shared_preferences: ^2.5.3
  
  # Device/Platform
  device_info_plus: ^11.5.0
  
  # Media
  audioplayers: ^5.2.1
  
  # Serialization
  json_annotation: ^4.8.1

dev_dependencies:
  # Testing
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
  
  # Code Generation
  build_runner: ^2.4.7
  json_serializable: ^6.7.1
  
  # Linting
  flutter_lints: ^3.0.1
```

**7. CI/CD INTEGRATION**
```yaml
# .github/workflows/dependencies.yml
name: Dependency Security Check
on: [push, pull_request, schedule]
jobs:
  security-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install OSV-Scanner
        run: go install github.com/google/osv-scanner/cmd/osv-scanner@latest
      - name: Run OSV-Scanner
        run: osv-scanner -r .
```

**8. DEPENDENCY CONFLICT RESOLUTION**
- **Override Strategy**: Use dependency_overrides sparingly
- **Version Constraints**: Adjust constraints to resolve conflicts
- **Alternative Packages**: Consider switching to compatible alternatives
- **Fork Strategy**: Fork and maintain packages if necessary

**9. PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS**
- **Bundle Size**: Monitor app size impact of new dependencies
- **Build Time**: Track compilation time increases
- **Runtime Performance**: Profile apps after major upgrades
- **Tree Shaking**: Ensure packages support tree shaking

**10. DOCUMENTATION AND TRACKING**
- **Changelog**: Maintain dependency upgrade log
- **Breaking Changes**: Document API changes and migration steps
- **Testing Notes**: Record test results for each upgrade
- **Rollback Plan**: Always have a rollback strategy

**11. TEAM COLLABORATION**
- **Lock File**: Always commit pubspec.lock to version control
- **Upgrade Coordination**: Coordinate major upgrades across team
- **Knowledge Sharing**: Document upgrade experiences
- **Code Reviews**: Include dependency changes in review process

**12. EMERGENCY PROCEDURES**
- **Security Patches**: Immediate upgrade process for critical vulnerabilities
- **Rollback Process**: Quick revert to last known good state
- **Communication**: Alert team of critical dependency issues
- **Hotfix Strategy**: Process for emergency dependency fixes

**RECOMMENDED TOOLS:**
- **OSV-Scanner**: Primary security scanning
- **Dependabot**: Automated updates
- **pub.dev**: Package research and evaluation
- **GitHub Advisory Database**: Vulnerability tracking
- **Flutter DevTools**: Performance monitoring post-upgrade
</info added on 2025-08-24T07:38:04.392Z>
